name: California Sunshine Bread
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup Orange juice, fresh (warmed to room temperature)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine lukewarm water, sugar, and dry yeast in a large bowl. Stir until completely dissolved.
2. Add warm orange juice and beat until well blended.
3. Gradually add 1 cup of flour, beating gently to make it smooth. Cover the bowl and set in a warm place until bubbly and light (30-40 minutes).
4. Add salt, margarine, and grated orange and lemon rinds; beat gently to mix.
5. Stir in the remaining flour gradually, mixing well. Turn onto a lightly floured board and knead until smooth and elastic (about 10 minutes).
6. Place in a large, oiled bowl, turning dough around to coat all over. Cover and place in a warm place until the dough has doubled in size (1-2 hours).
7. Punch down the dough in several places. Knead on the board for 5 minutes.
8. Shape into a loaf and place in an oiled 8 1/2" x 4 1/2" x 2 1/2" loaf pan. Cover and let rise in a warm place about 1 hour.
9. Preheat oven to 375 degrees. When the bread has risen, bake for 35-45 minutes.
10. Remove bread from the pan and cool on a wire rack.
Food Exchange per serving: 1 STARCH EXCHANGE PER SLICE (1 oz slice)
Low-sodium diets: Omit salt. Substitute salted margarine.
Source: The Art of Cooking for the Diabetic by Mary Abbott Hess, R.D., M.S. and Katharine Middleton
